name: GDPR centralised storage vs VN data localisation
explanation: GDPR requires that personal data of EU residents remains within the EEA or is transferred only under adequate safeguards (Art. 44–46). Vietnam's Cybersecurity Law §26 requires that important data about Vietnamese citizens is stored on servers physically located in Vietnam. A centralised data architecture cannot simultaneously satisfy both requirements: any single datastore is either in the EEA (violating VN law) or in Vietnam (violating GDPR transfer restrictions).

mitigationOptions.description: Operate separate data stores: EU-resident data remains in EEA-hosted infrastructure; Vietnamese-user data is stored on Vietnam-located servers. Implement SCC-based transfer layer for any necessary cross-border processing.
Launch in Vietnam without collecting or processing data subject to localisation requirements until architecture can be separated.

name: UK GDPR adequacy gap post-Brexit
explanation: The EU-UK adequacy decision is in place but subject to periodic review. Data flows between EU and UK require monitoring. UK GDPR is substantively similar to EU GDPR but is a separate legal instrument enforced by the ICO, not EU DPAs.

mitigationOptions.description: Implement EU-approved SCCs for EU→UK transfers as a belt-and-braces measure alongside the adequacy decision.

name: Germany PE risk from EOR headcount
explanation: Three or more employees working in Germany through an EOR creates a strong risk of permanent establishment (PE) under German tax law (§12 AO) and the OECD Model Tax Convention Art. 5. PE exposes the Swedish HQ to Körperschaftsteuer (German corporate income tax) on profits attributable to the German operations. The EOR structure does not reliably prevent PE when the business activity is sustained and the employees have authority to bind the company.

mitigationOptions.description: Incorporate a German subsidiary (GmbH). PE is replaced by a taxable entity, which is a known, manageable structure. Requires ~4–6 weeks and €25,000 share capital.
Engage German tax counsel to assess whether current activity constitutes PE and obtain a documented opinion.

name: Works council threshold trigger on expansion
explanation: The Betriebsverfassungsgesetz (BetrVG) §1 requires the establishment of a Works Council (Betriebsrat) when a workplace has 5 or more permanent employees. Once formed, the Works Council has co-determination rights over working hours, overtime, short-time work, holiday schedules, and health and safety measures. This is a threshold trigger — it fires at the point the 5th employee joins.

mitigationOptions.option: Plan for Works Council from day one
mitigationOptions.description: Design employment contracts, onboarding, and HR processes with Works Council co-determination rights in mind before the 5th hire.

name: GDPR special-category data heightened obligations
explanation: HR data frequently constitutes special-category data under GDPR Art. 9 (e.g. health data in sick leave records, union membership, biometric data). Processing special-category data requires an explicit legal basis under Art. 9(2), a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) under Art. 35, and appointment of a Data Protection Officer (DPO) if processing is carried out on a large scale or as a core activity.

mitigationOptions.description: Commission a Data Protection Impact Assessment as required by GDPR Art. 35 before any special-category HR data is processed.
Assess whether DPO appointment is mandatory under GDPR Art. 37. If yes, appoint before processing begins.
